content_en:
> Al-Hafiz al-Dhahabi said in a/-Siyar, 21/375 that this text is a part of some other text.

THE BIOGRAPHY OF THE AUTHOR

al-Hafiz Abt’l-Faraj ‘Abdu’l-Rahman Ibn Jawzi

His Name and Lineage

He is Abi’l-Faraj Jamal al-Din ‘Abdu’l-Rahman ibn ‘Ali ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Ali Ibn ‘Ubayd Allah Ibn al-Jawzi al-Qurashi alTamimi al-Bakdi from the family of Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr a/- Siddig, al-Baghdadi al-Hanbali.$

His Birth and Upbringing He was born in 509 or 510 A.H. Upon reaching adolescence, his aunt took him to Ibn Nasir from whom he learned a great

deal. He came to love preaching while barely having reached the age of puberty, and from then started to give sermons to the peo-

6 Thail al-Randatain, p.21, al-Bidayah wa'l-Nihayab, p. 13/26.

al-Hafiz Abu'l-Faraj ‘Abdu'l-Rahman thn Jawzi

His father passed away when he was three years old so his aunt took care of him. His telatives were copper merchants so at times in hadith hearings he would write his name as ‘Abdu’l-Rahman ibn ‘Ali al-Saffar [i.e. The Coppersmith].

His first hadith hearing was in 156 A.H., as cited by al-Dhahabi’

While still very young he became known as a religious person who would not socialise with anyone and would not eat from any food whose source was doubtful. He would only leave his house for prayer and he would not play with other kids. He was a person of very great determination and ambition and he spent all his life busy in seeking knowledge, preaching and authoring,*

Al-Hafiz Ibn al-Jawzi has already introduced his teachers in his book Mashyakhat Ibn al-Jawzi i.e. the scholars who taught Ibn alJawzi] where he listed many of them. In the field of hadith he benefitted from accompanying Ibn Nasir , in Qur’dan and Adab {ie. Manners] from Sibt al-Khiyat and Ibn al-Jawiliqi. He was the last to narrate from al-Dinawari and al-Mutawakkili.’

His Students Those who narrated from him include his son and companion,

” Thail al-Raudatain, 21, Thail ‘ala Tabagat al-Hanabila, 1/401, Sbatharat al-Thabab,

* al-Bidayab wa'lNibayah, 13/29, Said al-Khatir, 238. > Siyar al-A'lam al-Nubula’, 21/366, 367.

His grandson Abii al-Muzfir and majority of those who wrote his biography say that he had three sons:

1. The oldest of them, Abi Bakr ‘Abd al-‘Aziz: He became a jurist in the school of Ahmad and took knowledge from Abii al-Waqt, Ibn Nasir, al-Armawi and a group of his father's teachers. He traveled to the city of al-Musul where he preached and held sermons there for which he earned the people full acceptance. It is said that the family of alZahrazuti used to be jealous of him so they made someone put poison in his drink which caused him to die in al-Misul in 554 A.H. during the lifetime of his father."

2. Abii al-Qasim Badr al-Din ‘Ali al-Nasikh: A separate biography of him will follow. He is the one his father is addressing in this book.

3. Abi Muhammad Yasuf Muhyi al-Din'?: He was the most

"\ Tail Tabagat al-Hanabila, 1/430, 431.

alHafiz, Abit l-Faraj ‘Abdu’l-Rabman ibn Janz

intelligent and youngest son as he was born in 580 A.H. He became involved in preaching and held sermons after his father, in which he excelled and by which he was looked up to by his confreres. He then was appointed to control and supervise markets of Baghdad, and after that he was assigned to deliver the messages of the caliphs to kings of other different regions peculiarly to the Ayyabi family in the Sham region. He held the position of teacher in the institute of caliph al-Musti‘sim in 640 A.H. till he was killed in prison in 656 A.H. by Hulaku who occupied Bagdad and destroyed it. His three sons Jamal al-Din, Sharaf al-Din and Taj al-Din were killed with him. He has authored many works, including Ma‘adin al-Abriz fi Tafsir al-Kitab al-‘Aziz and al-Madhab al-Abmad fi Madhab Abmad. Unlike his brother Abu al-Qasim, he was a dutiful son who honored his father and treated him well.

His grandson mentioned that Ibn al-Jawzi had many daughters; Rabi‘a, Sharaf al-Nisa’, Zainab, Jauhara, Sitt al-Ulama al-Sughra and Sitt al-‘Ulama al-Kubra."*

His Uniqueness as a Preacher

Excellent indeed are the words of al-Hafiz al-Dhahabi about him: ‘He was the leading figure in reminding the people and had no equal. He would recite pleasant poetry and eloquent prose spontaneously and his fine words and moving speech was abundant. There has never been anyone like him, not before him and not after him. He is the carrier of the flag of exhortation in its various forms. He had a pleasant appearance and a good voice and his

talks had an impact on peoples’ hearts. His lifestyle in general was beautiful.’"* He also said: ‘I believe there will not be another one like him,’

Al-Hafiz Ibn Rajab said: ‘So we conclude that his gatherings of exhortation were one of a kind and nobody had heard of anything like them. They were gatherings of great benefit where the heedless would be reminded, the ignorant would come to know, the sinners would repent and the polytheists would become Muslims.”

His Works and Effects

Shaikh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah said in a/-Ajwiba al-Misriyyah: ‘Shaikh Abi’l-Faraj excelled in many sciences and has many writings to his account. He would write on many topics, and as I counted his works I found them to be over one thousand in number. Afterwards, I found out about other works as well.”

Having mentioned some of his books, al-Dhahabi said: ‘I don’t know of a scholar who has written what this man has.”

al-Hafiz, Aba’ |-Faraj ‘Abds'l-Rabman ibn Jaw

he researched their titles, their copies and copies that had been printed and put them in alphabetic order. Whoever wishes to know about these books should refer to this work, keeping in mind that many of the manuscripts mentioned therein by al-‘Alaji have now been printed.

Ibn al-Jawzi passed away on Friday the 12th of Ramadan in 597 A.H and was buried at Bab Harb.’

” For a more detailed biography of al-Hafiz Ibn al-Jawzi, refer to: Ibn Athir’s al-Kamil, 12/71, Sibt Ibn al-Jaw2i: Mirat al-Zanin, 8/481, al-Mundhiti: af Takmila, Biography #608, al-Bagghal: a/-Mashyakba, #140, Aba Shamma: Thail alRandatain, #21, Yon al-Sai: ab-Jami’, 9/65, bn Khalkan: al-Wafayat, 3/140, alDhahabi: al-‘Ihar, 4/297, Dawal al-Islim, 2/79, Tatbkirat al-Huffaz, 4/1342, Siyar al-A lim al-Nubuld’, 21/365, Yon Kathit: al-Bidayab wa’ lNibayab, 13/26, bn Rajab: Thail Tabagat al-Hanabila, 1/399, al-Jazari: Ghayat wa'l-Nibayab, 1/375, Siddiq Hasan Khan: al-Té al-Mukallal, #70, Ibn Shatti: Mukhlasar Tabagat al-Hanabila, p.42.

THE BIOGRAPHY OF THE SON

Abi’l-Qasim Badr al-Din ‘Ali ibn al-Jawzi

Name, Lineage and Birth He is Badr al-Din Aba’l-Qasim ‘Ali, the son of the Shaikh and Imam Abi’l-Faraj ‘Abdu’l-Rahman ibn ‘Ali ibn Muhammad ibn “Ali ibn al-Jawzi al-Baksi, al-Baghdadi al-Nasikh.

He was born in Ramadan in 551 A.H.” which was approximately 30 years before the birth of his brother Yusuf Muhyi al-Din.

He studied under Abi’l-Faraj al-Batti, Yahya ibn Thabit, Abi Zur‘a, Ahmad ibn al-Mugarrab, minister ibn Hubaira and Shuhda.

Al-Sayf narrated from him as well as ‘Izz al-Din ‘Abdu’l-Rahman ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Ghani al-Maqdisi, al-Taqj ibn al-Wasiti,

” Siar al-Aam al-Nubuld’, 22/352.

The Biography of the Son

al-Kamal ‘Ali ibn al-Waddah, Abi’l-Faraj ibn al-Zain, Abi al- ‘Abbas al-Farathi, Shams al-Din Muhammad ibn Hubaira the resident of Bilbeis. He gave permission to Abii Nast ibn al-Shirazi and al-Qadi the Hanbali to narrate from him.

Ibn Nugta said: ‘He relates with accuracy, is trustworthy, has memorized a lot and presents his hadith in a fine manner. He heard the Sahih of al-Isma‘ll from Yahya ibn Thabit.’””'

Upbringing and Life with his Father al-Imam ibn al-Jawzi

One may ask why Ibn al-Jawzi chose to advice this son of his, and the answer can be easily found by looking into their life together. The author says in the preface that having found his son somewhat careless about seeking knowledge, he decided to write some words of encouragment to him to get him motivated. The scholars’ comments about him give us the following picture of Abii’l-Qasim’s life with his father; al-Imam Ibn al-Jawzi:

He was a good child from the very beginning to the point that Ibn al-Najjar said: ‘He preached and gave sermons when he was a child.” Al-Dhahabi said: ‘He preached for some time and then quit. He was extraordinarily eloquent in much of his speech and pleasant in his jesting.”*’ This indicates that his upbringing and early childhood were like those of his father and his brother Muhyi al-Din Yisuf.

% Siyar al-A‘lim al-Nubula’, 22/352.

However, at some point before the birth of his brother Mubyi al-Din Yasuf in 580 A.H., he began to spend his days in idle play, leaving his previous activities as a preacher and wasting his time doing impermissible things. This is verified by al-Hafiz Ibn al-Jawzi himself in this advice as he says: ‘Out of my sons only Abi’lQasim is left, so I have asked Allah to make him a pious successor and an ideal son who shall achieve success. However, I found him somewhat careless of studying Sacred Knowledge so I decided to write these words to him.’

He gave this excellent and also obligatory advice to his only son at the time, following the words of Allah?:

Kubkosigus O Believers! Protect yourselves and your families from fire...

[Sarah at-Tabrim : 6}

Still, despite his efforts, it seems that his son did not take heed of his words which caused his father to abandon him and cut all relations between them. Some of the things that made him go to this extent were his son’s habit of engaging in impermissible activities and having friends who were busy with idle play and cortupt lifestyle. He had become a person inclined towards amusement and moral depravity.
content_id:
> Al-Hafiz al-Dhahabi berkata dalam al-Siyar, 21/375 bahwa teks ini merupakan bagian dari teks lain.

BIOGRAFI PENULIS

al-Hafiz Abu'l-Faraj 'Abdu'l-Rahman Ibn Jawzi

Nama dan Nasab Beliau

Beliau adalah Abu'l-Faraj Jamal al-Din 'Abdu'l-Rahman ibn 'Ali ibn Muhammad ibn 'Ali Ibn 'Ubayd Allah Ibn al-Jawzi al-Qurashi al-Tamimi al-Bakri dari keluarga Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr al-Siddiq, al-Baghdadi al-Hanbali.$

Kelahiran dan Pertumbuhan Beliau Beliau dilahirkan pada tahun 509 atau 510 H. Ketika menginjak usia remaja, bibinya membawanya kepada Ibn Nasir, dari siapa beliau belajar banyak

hal. Beliau mulai mencintai ceramah ketika hampir belum mencapai usia baligh, dan sejak saat itu mulai memberikan khutbah kepada orang-orang.

6 Thail al-Raudatain, hal. 21, al-Bidayah wa'l-Nihayah, hal. 13/26.

al-Hafiz Abu'l-Faraj 'Abdu'l-Rahman ibn Jawzi

Ayahnya wafat ketika beliau berusia tiga tahun sehingga bibinya yang merawat beliau. Kerabat-kerabatnya adalah pedagang tembaga, sehingga terkadang dalam majelis-majelis hadith beliau menulis namanya sebagai 'Abdu'l-Rahman ibn 'Ali al-Saffar [yaitu Tukang Tembaga].

Majelis hadith pertama beliau adalah pada tahun 156 H., sebagaimana dikutip oleh al-Dhahabi.

Sejak masih sangat muda, beliau sudah dikenal sebagai sosok yang taat beragama, tidak bergaul dengan sembarang orang dan tidak mau makan makanan yang sumbernya meragukan. Beliau hanya keluar rumah untuk salah dan tidak bermain dengan anak-anak lain. Beliau adalah pribadi yang sangat teguh tekad dan cita-citanya, menghabiskan seluruh hidupnya dalam menuntut ilmu, berdakwah dan mengarang kitab.*

Al-Hafiz Ibn al-Jawzi telah memperkenalkan guru-gurunya dalam kitabnya Mashyakhat Ibn al-Jawzi [yaitu para ulama yang mengajar Ibn al-Jawzi] di mana beliau menyebutkan banyak dari mereka. Dalam bidang hadith, beliau mendapat manfaat besar dari persahabatan dengan Ibn Nasir, dalam bidang Quran dan Adab [yaitu Akhlak] dari Sibt al-Khiyat dan Ibn al-Jawaliqi. Beliau adalah orang terakhir yang meriwayatkan dari al-Dinawari dan al-Mutawakkili.'

Murid-murid Beliau Mereka yang meriwayatkan darinya termasuk putra dan sahabatnya,

" Thail al-Raudatain, 21, Thail 'ala Tabaqat al-Hanabila, 1/401, Shadzarat al-Dzahab,

* al-Bidayah wa'l-Nihayah, 13/29, Said al-Khatir, 238. > Siyar al-A'lam al-Nubula', 21/366, 367.

Cucunya Abu al-Muzaffar dan mayoritas penulis biografinya menyebutkan bahwa beliau memiliki tiga orang putra:

1. Yang tertua, Abu Bakr 'Abd al-'Aziz: Beliau menjadi ahli fiqh dalam madhab Ahmad dan mengambil ilmu dari Abu al-Waqt, Ibn Nasir, al-Armawi dan sekelompok guru ayahnya. Beliau melakukan perjalanan ke kota al-Mushul di mana beliau berdakwah dan berceramah di sana yang membuatnya mendapat penerimaan penuh dari masyarakat. Dikatakan bahwa keluarga al-Zahrazuti merasa iri kepadanya sehingga mereka menyuruh seseorang menaruh racun dalam minumannya yang menyebabkan beliau wafat di al-Mushul pada tahun 554 H. semasa hidup ayahnya."

2. Abu al-Qasim Badr al-Din 'Ali al-Nasikh: Biografi tersendiri tentangnya akan menyusul. Dialah yang dituju oleh ayahnya dalam kitab ini.

3. Abu Muhammad Yusuf Muhyi al-Din': Beliau adalah yang paling

"\ Thail Tabaqat al-Hanabila, 1/430, 431.

al-Hafiz Abu'l-Faraj 'Abdu'l-Rahman ibn Jawzi

cerdas dan putra bungsu karena beliau lahir pada tahun 580 H. Beliau terjun dalam bidang dakwah dan berceramah setelah ayahnya, di mana beliau unggul dan karenanya diteladani oleh rekan-rekan sejawatnya. Kemudian beliau ditunjuk untuk mengawasi dan mengontrol pasar-pasar Baghdad, dan setelah itu beliau ditugaskan untuk menyampaikan pesan-pesan khalifah kepada raja-raja di berbagai wilayah lain, terutama kepada keluarga Ayyubi di wilayah Syam. Beliau memegang jabatan pengajar di lembaga Khalifah al-Musta'sim pada tahun 640 H. hingga beliau terbunuh di penjara pada tahun 656 H. oleh Hulaku yang menduduki Baghdad dan menghancurkannya. Tiga putranya, Jamal al-Din, Sharaf al-Din dan Taj al-Din, terbunuh bersamanya. Beliau memiliki banyak karya tulis, di antaranya Ma'adin al-Ibriz fi Tafsir al-Kitab al-'Aziz dan al-Madzhab al-Ahmad fi Madzhab Ahmad. Berbeda dengan saudaranya Abu al-Qasim, beliau adalah putra yang berbakti yang menghormati ayahnya dan memperlakukan beliau dengan baik.

Cucunya menyebutkan bahwa Ibn al-Jawzi memiliki banyak putri; Rabi'a, Sharaf al-Nisa', Zainab, Jauhara, Sitt al-'Ulama al-Sughra dan Sitt al-'Ulama al-Kubra."*

Keistimewaan Beliau Sebagai Penceramah

Sungguh indah ucapan al-Hafiz al-Dhahabi tentang beliau: 'Beliau adalah tokoh terdepan dalam mengingatkan manusia dan tidak ada yang menyamainya. Beliau membacakan syair yang indah dan prosa yang fasih secara spontan, dan kata-kata halus serta pidato yang menggerakkan hati sangat melimpah darinya. Tidak pernah ada seorang pun seperti beliau, tidak sebelumnya dan tidak sesudahnya. Beliau adalah pembawa panji nasihat dalam berbagai bentuknya. Beliau memiliki penampilan yang menarik dan suara yang merdu, dan

ceramah-ceramahnya memiliki pengaruh yang mendalam terhadap hati manusia. Gaya hidupnya secara umum sangat indah.'"* Beliau juga berkata: 'Aku yakin tidak akan ada lagi orang sepertinya,'

Al-Hafiz Ibn Rajab berkata: 'Maka kami menyimpulkan bahwa majelis-majelis nasihatnya adalah satu-satunya dan tidak ada seorang pun yang pernah mendengar sesuatu yang seperti itu. Majelis-majelis tersebut sangat bermanfaat di mana orang-orang yang lalai diingatkan, orang-orang yang jahil mendapat pengetahuan, para pendosa bertaubat dan orang-orang musyrik masuk Islam."

Karya-karya dan Peninggalan Beliau

Syaikhul Islam Ibn Taymiyyah berkata dalam al-Ajwiba al-Misriyyah: 'Syaikh Abu'l-Faraj unggul dalam banyak bidang ilmu dan memiliki banyak tulisan. Beliau menulis dalam berbagai topik, dan ketika aku menghitung karya-karyanya, aku mendapati jumlahnya lebih dari seribu. Setelah itu, aku menemukan karya-karya lainnya juga."

Setelah menyebutkan beberapa kitabnya, al-Dhahabi berkata: 'Aku tidak mengetahui seorang ulama pun yang telah menulis sebanyak apa yang ditulis orang ini."

al-Hafiz Abu'l-Faraj 'Abdu'l-Rahman ibn Jawzi

beliau meneliti judul-judulnya, salinan-salinannya dan salinan yang telah dicetak lalu menyusunnya dalam urutan abjad. Siapa pun yang ingin mengetahui tentang kitab-kitab ini hendaknya merujuk pada karya tersebut, dengan catatan bahwa banyak manuskrip yang disebutkan di dalamnya oleh al-'Alaji kini telah dicetak.

Ibn al-Jawzi wafat pada hari Jumat tanggal 12 Ramadan tahun 597 H. dan dimakamkan di Bab Harb.'

" Untuk biografi yang lebih rinci tentang al-Hafiz Ibn al-Jawzi, lihat: Ibn Atsir: al-Kamil, 12/71, Sibt Ibn al-Jawzi: Mir'at al-Zaman, 8/481, al-Mundziri: al-Takmila, Biografi #608, al-Baqqal: al-Mashyakha, #140, Abu Shama: Thail al-Raudatain, #21, Ibn al-Sa'i: al-Jami', 9/65, Ibn Khallikan: al-Wafayat, 3/140, al-Dhahabi: al-'Ibar, 4/297, Duwal al-Islam, 2/79, Tadzkirat al-Huffaz, 4/1342, Siyar al-A'lam al-Nubula', 21/365, Ibn Katsir: al-Bidayah wa'l-Nihayah, 13/26, Ibn Rajab: Thail Tabaqat al-Hanabila, 1/399, al-Jazari: Ghayat wa'l-Nihayah, 1/375, Siddiq Hasan Khan: al-Taj al-Mukallal, #70, Ibn Shatti: Mukhtasar Tabaqat al-Hanabila, hal. 42.

BIOGRAFI SANG PUTRA

Abu'l-Qasim Badr al-Din 'Ali ibn al-Jawzi

Nama, Nasab dan Kelahiran Beliau adalah Badr al-Din Abu'l-Qasim 'Ali, putra dari Syaikh dan Imam Abu'l-Faraj 'Abdu'l-Rahman ibn 'Ali ibn Muhammad ibn 'Ali ibn al-Jawzi al-Bakri, al-Baghdadi al-Nasikh.

Beliau dilahirkan pada bulan Ramadan tahun 551 H." yaitu kurang lebih 30 tahun sebelum kelahiran saudaranya Yusuf Muhyi al-Din.

Beliau belajar kepada Abu'l-Faraj al-Batti, Yahya ibn Tsabit, Abu Zur'a, Ahmad ibn al-Muqarrab, menteri ibn Hubaira dan Shuhda.

Al-Saif meriwayatkan darinya, begitu juga 'Izz al-Din 'Abdu'l-Rahman ibn Muhammad ibn 'Abd al-Ghani al-Maqdisi, al-Taqi ibn al-Wasiti,

" Siyar al-A'lam al-Nubula', 22/352.

Biografi Sang Putra

al-Kamal 'Ali ibn al-Waddah, Abu'l-Faraj ibn al-Zain, Abu al-'Abbas al-Farutsi, Shams al-Din Muhammad ibn Hubaira penduduk Bilbeis. Beliau memberikan ijazah kepada Abu Nashr ibn al-Shirazi dan al-Qadi bermadzhab Hanbali untuk meriwayatkan darinya.

Ibn Nuqta berkata: 'Beliau meriwayatkan dengan akurat, dapat dipercaya, memiliki banyak hafalan dan menyajikan hadith-nya dengan cara yang baik. Beliau mendengar Sahih al-Isma'ili dari Yahya ibn Tsabit.'"'

Pertumbuhan dan Kehidupan Bersama Ayahnya al-Imam ibn al-Jawzi

Seseorang mungkin bertanya mengapa Ibn al-Jawzi memilih untuk menasihati putranya yang satu ini, dan jawabannya dapat dengan mudah ditemukan dengan melihat kehidupan mereka bersama. Sang penulis berkata dalam mukadimah bahwa setelah mendapati putranya agak lalai dalam menuntut ilmu, beliau memutuskan untuk menulis beberapa kata dorongan kepadanya agar termotivasi. Komentar-komentar para ulama tentangnya memberikan gambaran berikut tentang kehidupan Abu'l-Qasim bersama ayahnya; al-Imam Ibn al-Jawzi:

Beliau adalah anak yang baik sejak awal hingga Ibn al-Najjar berkata: 'Beliau berdakwah dan berceramah ketika masih kecil." Al-Dhahabi berkata: 'Beliau berceramah untuk beberapa waktu kemudian berhenti. Beliau sangat fasih dalam banyak ucapannya dan menyenangkan dalam gurauan.'*' Ini menunjukkan bahwa pengasuhan dan masa kecilnya serupa dengan ayahnya dan saudaranya Muhyi al-Din Yusuf.

% Siyar al-A'lam al-Nubula', 22/352.

Namun, pada suatu titik sebelum kelahiran saudaranya Muhyi al-Din Yusuf pada tahun 580 H., beliau mulai menghabiskan hari-harinya dalam permainan yang sia-sia, meninggalkan aktivitas sebelumnya sebagai penceramah dan menyia-nyiakan waktunya dalam hal-hal yang diharamkan. Hal ini dibenarkan oleh al-Hafiz Ibn al-Jawzi sendiri dalam nasihat ini ketika beliau berkata: 'Di antara putra-putraku hanya Abu'l-Qasim yang tersisa, maka aku memohon kepada Allah agar menjadikannya penerus yang saleh dan putra yang teladan yang akan meraih keberhasilan. Namun, aku mendapatinya agak lalai dalam mempelajari Ilmu Agama sehingga aku memutuskan untuk menulis kata-kata ini untuknya.'

Beliau memberikan nasihat yang sangat baik dan juga wajib ini kepada satu-satunya putranya pada waktu itu, mengikuti firman Allah:

Wahai orang-orang yang beriman! Peliharalah dirimu dan keluargamu dari api neraka...

[Surah at-Tahrim: 6]

Namun demikian, meskipun dengan segala upayanya, tampaknya putranya tidak mengindahkan kata-katanya yang menyebabkan sang ayah meninggalkannya dan memutus semua hubungan di antara mereka. Beberapa hal yang membuatnya sampai pada tingkat tersebut adalah kebiasaan putranya terlibat dalam aktivitas yang diharamkan dan memiliki teman-teman yang sibuk dengan permainan sia-sia dan gaya hidup yang rusak. Dia telah menjadi sosok yang condong pada hiburan dan kemerosotan moral.
